intent: In DATA_DIR there is a 10x matrix triplet for one PBMC sample — files prefixed GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1 (matrix.mtx.gz, barcodes.tsv.gz, features.tsv.gz). Using scanpy, do standard QC, normalization, highly-variable genes, PCA, neighbors, Leiden clustering and a UMAP, then identify marker genes per cluster and show me a UMAP and a marker dotplot.

blocks.text: Perfect. Now I'll execute the workflow on the GSM5746268 sample. This is a standard PBMC sample, so I'll use the human PBMC defaults (pct_mt < 20, n_genes >= 200).

input.code:
import scanpy as sc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import os
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Set figure output directory
sc.settings.figdir = '.'

# Load the 10x triplet for GSM5746268
D = os.environ['DATA_DIR']
pre = "GSM5746268_MGI0369_1_SLAB-C1-C1"

# Read the matrix triplet
adata = sc.read_mtx(f"{D}/{pre}.matrix.mtx.gz").T  # mtx is genes x cells, transpose to cells x genes
adata.obs_names = pd.read_csv(f"{D}/{pre}.barcodes.tsv.gz", header=None)[0].values
adata.var_names = pd.read_csv(f"{D}/{pre}.features.tsv.gz", header=None, sep='\t')[1].values  # col 2 = gene symbols
adata.var_names_make_unique()

print(f"Raw data shape: {adata.shape}")
print(f"First few barcodes: {list(adata.obs_names[:3])}")
print(f"First few genes: {list(adata.var_names[:3])}")
print(f"Gene name examples: {list(adata.var_names[::500][:5])}")
